name: Sour Milk Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the dry ingredients (except the sugar) three times.
2. Cream the sugar and lard well, then add the eggs. Beat with an electric mixer until smooth.
3. Add the sour milk or buttermilk and beat again until combined.
4. Gradually add the sifted dry ingredients to the wet mixture and mix thoroughly.
5. Cover the batter and chill for 1 hour.
6. Divide the dough into two parts. Roll each on a floured board to a thickness of 1/2 inch.
7. Cut the rolled dough with a doughnut cutter using a sharp knife or cookie cutter.
8. Melt the lard in a deep fryer and heat to 375°F (190°C).
9. Fry the doughnuts quickly, turning them as soon as they rise to the surface of the oil to avoid overcrowding the pot.
10. Cook until golden brown on all sides.
11. If desired, roll the hot doughnuts in granulated sugar while still warm.
12. Strain the used lard through a fine mesh, cover, and store in the refrigerator for future use.
Makes about 2 dozen doughnuts.
